summary.intent: Add an E2E test reproducing GitHub issue #732 (MCP servers incorrectly showing 'dead' status) and plan a fix for the root cause in the SSE transport health check.
summary.outcome: Created a mock MCP HTTP server, added 5 Rust unit tests demonstrating the buggy is_alive() behavior, added a Playwright E2E test using the mock server, and produced a detailed fix plan. All 93 Rust tests pass and linting is clean. Committed, pushed, and opened a PR.

repo: MCP health status is determined in manager.rs by calling c.is_alive() on Ready-state clients, mapping true→'running' and false→'dead'.
The gateway E2E test pattern uses a mock OAuth server (mock-oauth-server.js) started as a child process in base-test.js; new mock servers should follow the same pattern.
The UI adds MCP servers via an RPC call pattern handled in page-mcp.js, with a Streamable HTTP form that accepts a URL and Bearer token.
Pre-existing rustfmt violations exist in unrelated files (config/validate, providers) on the main branch — format check on changed files only to avoid noise.
The project uses a 'beads' issue tracker alongside GitHub; tasks are claimed before implementation begins.

code.finding: SseTransport::is_alive() sends a GET request but only treats 2xx and 401 as alive. GET is optional in MCP Streamable HTTP spec — servers may return 405/403/400, which this code incorrectly maps to 'dead'. Fix: treat any HTTP response as alive; only network errors = dead.
McpClientState::Ready branch calls is_alive() to determine 'running' vs 'dead' display string — this is the single callsite affected by the bug.

workflow: Writing bug-demonstrating unit tests that assert current broken behavior (with flip-after-fix comments) is an effective way to document bugs and guide reviewers.
Using mockito for Rust HTTP mocking and a lightweight Node.js script for E2E HTTP mocking covers both layers without heavy dependencies.
Running cargo test on the specific crate (not workspace-wide) is faster for iteration during test development.

friction: JS linting required multiple attempts to invoke correctly (wrong working directory on first tries before finding the repo root invocation).
rustfmt check produced noisy diffs from pre-existing violations in unrelated files, requiring a targeted per-file check to confirm the changed file was clean.
open_items: The actual one-line fix to is_alive() was planned but not applied — tests currently assert the buggy behavior and must be flipped after the fix is implemented.
The E2E test assertion uses toContain(['running', 'dead']) as a temporary allowance; it should be tightened to toBe('running') once the fix lands.
Broader integration testing with @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything (official MCP test server) was identified but not wired up.
